text.title: Cache Hit Audit Script Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the cache-hit-audit-script methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Cache audit MUST run as a daily cron (UTC). Manual on-demand runs are allowed but the daily report is the authoritative log.

rationale.source: Anthropic engineering blog — cache best practices

rule.rationale: Cache regressions can emerge after any deploy; daily cadence catches them before the monthly bill.

rule.statement: Audit MUST cluster API calls by SHA-256 of the cached prefix (system prompt + tool definitions). Per-cluster cache ratio is computed independently.
rule.rationale: Aggregate cache ratio hides per-prefix regressions; clustering surfaces the specific prompt that broke.

rule.statement: Report MUST flag any prefix whose cache_read ratio dropped > 20pp from the previous day AND whose call volume ≥ 100/day.
rule.rationale: Below 20pp delta is within typical traffic-mix variance; below 100 calls/day is too noisy for stable measurement.

rule.statement: The audit MUST verify that no flagged prefix contains per-user / per-session variables (user_id, session_id, timestamps) interpolated into the system prompt. Flagging is automatic via regex + size-vs-prev-run delta.
rule.rationale: Per-user variables in the system prompt are the #1 cache-poisoning cause; auto-flag catches it before manual review.

rule.statement: Alert MUST fire when total cache_read_tokens / total_input_tokens (over 24h) drops > 15pp from 7-day rolling average.
rule.rationale: Aggregate signal catches systemic issues (provider outage, library upgrade) that per-prefix flagging would miss.
